ddls:
CREATE TABLE "languages" (
  "language_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each language */,
  "language_code" TEXT /* The code for the language (e.g., en, fr, es) */,
  "language_name" TEXT /* The name of the language */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("language_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "translations" (
  "translation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each translation */,
  "message_id" INTEGER /* The ID of the message being translated */,
  "language_id" INTEGER /* The ID of the language to which the message is being translated */,
  "translated_text" TEXT /* The translated text of the message */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("translation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_translations_language_id FOREIGN KEY ("language_id") REFERENCES languages ("language_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., aid worker, coordinator, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* The timestamp when the user account was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "response_logs" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response */,
  "message_id" INTEGER /* ID of the message being responded to */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user responding to the message */,
  "response_date" TEXT /* Date when the response was made */,
  "response_type" TEXT /* Type of response (e.g., aid provided, information given) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the response (e.g., pending, completed) */,
  "response_text" TEXT /* The text of the response */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_response_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* The name of the role */,
  "permission" TEXT /* The permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* The name of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "role_permissions" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* The ID of the role */,
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* The ID of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id", "permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytic entry */,
  "message_id" INTEGER /* The ID of the message being analyzed */,
  "response_rate" REAL /* The rate at which responses were made */,
  "response_time" REAL /* The average time taken to respond */,
  "aid_distribution" REAL /* The distribution of aid */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytic_id")
)
